hooks.name: Steering change suggests re-audit
hooks.description: Reminds the assistant that when a steering rule changes, consumers of that rule likely drift. Suggests invoking standards-steward on the affected modules.

action.prompt:
A steering file at .kiro/steering/ was just saved.

If the change is a genuine rule change (new invariant, tightened constraint, renamed concept, removed exception) rather than a trivial edit (typo fix, reformat, prose polish), consider whether existing consumers now drift from the new rule.

The right handoff:

- Invoke standards-steward (.kiro/agents/standards-steward.md) on the modules that consume this rule to surface drift.
- If the drift is mechanical (docblocks, attribute migrations, folder placement, column constants), hand the steward's findings to codebase-housekeeper (.kiro/agents/codebase-housekeeper.md).
- If the rule change deserves an ADR, invoke docs-adr-steward (.kiro/agents/docs-adr-steward.md) to author it.

Skip this reminder for trivial edits. Match the response depth to the change's scope.
